intro: Long distance is hard — but some bonds are built for it. Answer 7 honest questions for couples doing the distance and get an honest snapshot of your bond right now (a snapshot, never a verdict).

options.label: Like a ritual we never skip

questions.text: The next time you'll see each other is…

options.label: We've built around it — it barely bites
We handle it with some juggling
It regularly eats our call time

questions.text: When jealousy or doubt shows up, you…

options.label: Say it out loud early — it dissolves
Talk it through within a day or two
Sit on it a while before raising it

questions.text: Your shared rituals (a question, a photo, a show)…
The end of the distance is…

options.label: A real plan with a date
A shared goal we talk about

unshakable.title: Unshakable
unshakable.description: Distance is just geography for you two — rituals, trust, and a plan hold it all together. You've turned the hard mode into your normal.
unshakable.tip: Protect the rituals; they're the whole engine. The day they slip is the day the distance starts to feel long.

thriving.title: Thriving Apart
thriving.description: You've made the distance work with real habits and steady contact. The bond is strong and the effort shows.
thriving.tip: One upgrade: put the next reunion date somewhere you both see daily. A countdown does quiet, powerful work.

building.title: Building the Bridge
building.description: The love is clearly there; the systems are still catching up. You're closer than the logistics make it feel.
building.tip: Pick ONE daily ritual — a message, a photo, a question — and guard it for 30 days. Consistency beats intensity.

stretched.title: Stretched Thin
stretched.description: The distance is taking a toll right now — and that's a signal, not a sentence. Naming it is the first repair.
stretched.tip: Talk about the ritual you miss most, and book the next reunion. A date on the calendar changes how the weeks feel.
